old_claim.note: Paper Making provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Paper notebooks supplied the writable medium for the large serial archive of consultation records that defines this scoped casebook practice.

source_shape.source_locator: The critical edition describes 66 handwritten volumes containing roughly 80,000 consultations and dates the surviving Forman and Napier casebook runs.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The anchor practice is materially embodied in a large handwritten notebook archive rather than only in oral consultation or printed anatomy.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source upgrades a generic medium inference by identifying the physical serial volumes that preserve and define the scoped record-keeping practice.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: paper_making remains an enabling medium rather than a medical method; changed: the edge now has archive-specific review evidence.
would_reject_if: The node is broadened to include oral case transmission or non-paper records for which paper production is not enabling.
A manuscript study shows that the anchor volumes used a different writing medium.
short_reason: The Cambridge edition documents the handwritten serial volumes that materially carry the casebook archive.
